question: What techniques exist for self-improving agents without fine-tuning?

heuristic_evaluation.question: What techniques exist for self-improving agents without fine-tuning?

coverage.aspect: limits of self-improvement without external feedback

claim_lines: Self-improving agents without fine-tuning usually use **test-time loops** that change text, prompts, memory, or workflows rather than model weights: verbal feedback/reflection (Reflexion), self-feedback/self-refinement (Self-Refine), prompt optimization (OPRO), and workflow optimization/search (AFlow). https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366 https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.17651 https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.03409 https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.10762
- **verbal feedback** — Reflexion explicitly uses linguistic feedback, reflective text, and episodic memory instead of weight updates: https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366
- **reflection** — Self-Refine has the same LLM produce feedback about its own output and then refine itself iteratively: https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.17651
- **self-refinement** — Self-Refine is a standalone self-refinement loop at test time, not a fine-tuning method: https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.17651

- **prompt or workflow optimization** — OPRO optimizes prompts via evaluate-and-iterate loops, and AFlow optimizes workflows via search plus execution feedback: https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.03409 https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.10762

- **limits of self-improvement without external feedback** — intrinsic self-correction can fail or degrade reasoning, and self-refinement can amplify self-bias unless external feedback is accurate: https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.01798 https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.11436

coverage_audit.reason: Explicitly identifies Reflexion’s linguistic/verbal feedback loop and ties it to no-weight-update self-improvement.
Describes Self-Refine as iterative self-feedback and refinement, which squarely addresses reflection.
Directly names Self-Refine as a test-time self-refinement loop without fine-tuning.
Covers both prompt optimization (OPRO) and workflow optimization/search (AFlow), satisfying the aspect well.

coverage_audit.aspect: limits of self-improvement without external feedback
coverage_audit.reason: States that self-correction can fail/degrade reasoning and that self-bias can be amplified without accurate external feedback, which is a relevant limitation.

citation_audit.claim: Self-improving agents without fine-tuning usually use test-time loops that change text, prompts, memory, or workflows rather than model weights: verbal feedback/reflection (Reflexion), self-feedback/self-refinement (Self-Refine), prompt optimization (OPRO), and workflow optimization/search (AFlow).
citation_audit.reason: The cited papers plausibly support the named techniques and the general test-time-loop framing.

citation_audit.claim: Reflexion explicitly uses linguistic feedback, reflective text, and episodic memory instead of weight updates.
citation_audit.reason: This is the core framing of Reflexion and is plausibly supported by the paper.

citation_audit.claim: Self-Refine has the same LLM produce feedback about its own output and then refine itself iteratively.
citation_audit.reason: This matches the paper’s self-feedback/refinement loop.

citation_audit.claim: Self-Refine is a standalone self-refinement loop at test time, not a fine-tuning method.
citation_audit.reason: Plausibly supported by the paper’s method description.

citation_audit.claim: OPRO optimizes prompts via evaluate-and-iterate loops, and AFlow optimizes workflows via search plus execution feedback.
citation_audit.reason: These are appropriate source choices for prompt and workflow optimization techniques.

citation_audit.claim: Intrinsic self-correction can fail or degrade reasoning, and self-refinement can amplify self-bias unless external feedback is accurate.
citation_audit.reason: These citations are relevant negative evidence about self-correction limits and self-bias.

source_audit.reason: Primary paper for Reflexion; directly relevant to verbal feedback and reflection-based self-improvement.
Primary Self-Refine paper; directly relevant to self-refinement and self-feedback.
Primary optimization paper for prompt-level self-improvement.
Relevant negative evidence on limits of self-correction without stronger feedback.
Relevant evidence on self-bias risks in self-refinement.
Relevant agent design/optimization paper for workflow search and optimization.

judge_evaluation.summary: A strong, well-cited answer that covers the required techniques and includes meaningful limits, with only minor room for more nuance on the distinction between reflection and self-refinement.
judge_evaluation.recommended_fix: Add one sentence clarifying that these methods improve behavior only at test time or through prompt/workflow changes, and briefly note that gains are task- and feedback-quality-dependent.
